comments.text:
## Design Review Rationale — 2026-07-15

The renderer child now records the current computed hierarchy instead of merely saying proportional. That gives the implementer a compatibility target: 14px must remain visually unchanged while other values scale from one article-level base.

The important trap is nested relative sizing. Inline code should remain about 0.85em of surrounding prose, but fenced code must not receive one ratio on `pre` and another on nested `code`. Table headers and task-list labels also need explicit review because their current sizes/inheritance differ from ordinary paragraphs.

Line height is part of usable font scaling even though the preference is named font size. Keeping `leading-6` as fixed 24px would clip or crowd 72px prose, so proportional/unitless line heights are required. Padding and spacing remain fixed by default because this is not general UI scaling; only demonstrated clipping may justify a narrow correction.

The base belongs at the shared Markdown article seam. That produces leverage across descriptions, comments, and preview and preserves one safety implementation. A preview-only renderer or per-element props would be shallow and likely drift in HTML/link/GFM behavior.

The widened 8-72 range expands accessibility without changing the default. Component tests own ratio and safety detail; desktop acceptance only samples computed styles in the assembled app.

## Implementation plan for bsm-al6.2

1. Extend the `MarkdownContent` component interface
   - Add an optional `fontSizePx?: number` prop.
   - Default to `14` when omitted so existing callers keep the current design.
   - Apply the base size at the single `article` seam with an inline CSS custom property (`--markdown-font-size`) and a `font-size` style. No dynamic Tailwind class names.

2. Replace fixed Tailwind font-size classes with `em` ratios relative to the article base
   - Body paragraphs, lists, blockquotes, table body, and h4: `1em`.
   - h1: `1.4286em`, h2: `1.2857em`, h3: `1.1429em`.
   - Inline code: `0.85em` of its surrounding text.
   - Fenced code blocks (`pre`): `0.8571em`; nested `code` inherits the same size and only receives background/padding resets.
   - Table header metadata (`th`): `0.7143em`.

3. Make line heights proportional
   - Body/list/paragraph line-height becomes a unitless ratio (`1.7143`) instead of the fixed `leading-6`/`24px`, so 72 px text does not clip.
   - Headings and code keep readable proportional line heights.

4. Preserve non-typography behavior
   - Keep `skipHtml`, GFM, HTTP-only external links, inert non-HTTP links, `noopener noreferrer`, task-list checkbox styling, code colors, and overflow behavior for code blocks and tables.
   - Do not scale any Issue Detail chrome (title, metadata, section labels, sidebar, Workspace controls).

5. Wire the prop through `IssueExplorer`
   - Add `markdownFontSizePx?: number` to `IssueExplorer` props and pass it to both the Issue description and the comment `MarkdownContent` call sites.
   - This proves description and comments share the same renderer interface while leaving persistence/app-shell state to bsm-al6.3.

6. Tests
   - In `MarkdownContent.test.tsx`:
     - omitted prop defaults to 14 px on the `article`;
     - explicit `8`, `14`, `24`, and `72` are reflected on the `article` style;
     - heading/code/table-header sizes are relative (not fixed pixel classes);
     - body/list line-height is proportional;
     - fenced `code` does not compound sizes;
     - existing HTML suppression, GFM, links, checkboxes remain unchanged.
   - In `IssueExplorer.test.tsx`:
     - `markdownFontSizePx` is forwarded to both description and comment `MarkdownContent` renderers.

7. Verification
   - `pnpm test`
   - `tsc --noEmit` (via `pnpm build` or `npx tsc`)
   - `pnpm dlx ultracite check`
   - `pnpm run build` (frontend)
   - React Doctor regression scan

No backend/persistence changes in this slice.

title: Make Markdown typography scale from a configurable base size
